class.name: Socket
class.signature: public class Socket extends Object implements Closeable
class.description: This class implements client sockets (also called just "sockets"). A socket is an endpoint for communication between two machines. The actual work of the socket is performed by an instance of the SocketImpl class. The Socket class defines convenience methods to set and get several socket options. This class also defines the setOption and getOption methods to set and query socket options. A Socket support the following options: Socket options Option Name Description SO_SNDBUF The size of the socket send buffer SO_RCVBUF The size of the socket receive buffer SO_KEEPALIVE Keep connection alive SO_REUSEADDR Re-use address SO_LINGER Linger on close if data is present (when configured in blocking mode only) TCP_NODELAY Disable the Nagle algorithm Additional (implementation specific) options may also be supported.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.description: Creates an unconnected Socket. If the application has specified a client socket implementation factory, that factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Otherwise a system-default socket implementation is created.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.description: Creates an unconnected socket, specifying the type of proxy, if any, that should be used regardless of any other settings. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the proxy host address and port number as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException. Examples: Socket s = new Socket(Proxy.NO_PROXY); will create a plain socket ignoring any other proxy configuration. Socket s = new Socket(new Proxy(Proxy.Type.SOCKS, new InetSocketAddress("socks.mydom.com", 1080))); will create a socket connecting through the specified SOCKS proxy server.

parameters.name: proxy
parameters.description: a Proxy object specifying what kind of proxying should be used.

throws.description: if the proxy is of an invalid type or null.
if a security manager is present and permission to connect to the proxy is denied.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.description: Creates an unconnected Socket with a user-specified SocketImpl.

parameters.name: impl
parameters.description: an instance of a SocketImpl the subclass wishes to use on the Socket.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.
if impl is non-null and a security manager is set and its checkPermission method doesn't allow NetPermission("setSocketImpl").

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: public Socket(String host, int port) throws UnknownHostException, IOException
constructors.description: Creates a stream socket and connects it to the specified port number on the named host. If the specified host is null it is the equivalent of specifying the address as InetAddress.getByName(null). In other words, it is equivalent to specifying an address of the loopback interface. If the application has specified a client socket implementation factory, that factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Otherwise a system-default socket implementation is created. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the host address and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: the host name, or null for the loopback address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the port number.

throws.description: if the IP address of the host could not be determined.
if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the operation.
if the port parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: public Socket(InetAddress address, int port) throws IOException
constructors.description: Creates a stream socket and connects it to the specified port number at the specified IP address. If the application has specified a client socket implementation factory, that factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Otherwise a system-default socket implementation is created. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the host address and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: the IP address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the port number.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the operation.
if the port parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.
if address is null.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: public Socket(String host, int port, InetAddress localAddr, int localPort) throws IOException
constructors.description: Creates a socket and connects it to the specified remote host on the specified remote port. The Socket will also bind() to the local address and port supplied. If the specified host is null it is the equivalent of specifying the address as InetAddress.getByName(null). In other words, it is equivalent to specifying an address of the loopback interface. A local port number of zero will let the system pick up a free port in the bind operation. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the host address and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: the name of the remote host, or null for the loopback address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the remote port

parameters.name: localAddr
parameters.description: the local address the socket is bound to, or null for the anyLocal address.

parameters.name: localPort
parameters.description: the local port the socket is bound to, or zero for a system selected free port.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the connection to the destination, or if its checkListen method doesn't allow the bind to the local port.
if the port parameter or localPort parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: public Socket(InetAddress address, int port, InetAddress localAddr, int localPort) throws IOException
constructors.description: Creates a socket and connects it to the specified remote address on the specified remote port. The Socket will also bind() to the local address and port supplied. If the specified local address is null it is the equivalent of specifying the address as the AnyLocal address (see InetAddress.isAnyLocalAddress()). A local port number of zero will let the system pick up a free port in the bind operation. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the host address and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: the remote address

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the remote port

parameters.name: localAddr
parameters.description: the local address the socket is bound to, or null for the anyLocal address.

parameters.name: localPort
parameters.description: the local port the socket is bound to or zero for a system selected free port.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the connection to the destination, or if its checkListen method doesn't allow the bind to the local port.
if the port parameter or localPort parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.
if address is null.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: @Deprecated public Socket(String host, int port, boolean stream) throws IOException
constructors.description: Creates a stream socket and connects it to the specified port number on the named host. If the specified host is null it is the equivalent of specifying the address as InetAddress.getByName(null). In other words, it is equivalent to specifying an address of the loopback interface. If the stream argument is true, this creates a stream socket. If the stream argument is false, it creates a datagram socket. If the application has specified a client socket implementation factory, that factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Otherwise a system-default socket implementation is created. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with the host address and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException. If a UDP socket is used, TCP/IP related socket options will not apply.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: the host name, or null for the loopback address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the port number.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: a boolean indicating whether this is a stream socket or a datagram socket.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the operation.
if the port parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

constructors.name: Socket
constructors.signature: @Deprecated public Socket(InetAddress host, int port, boolean stream) throws IOException
constructors.description: Creates a socket and connects it to the specified port number at the specified IP address. If the stream argument is true, this creates a stream socket. If the stream argument is false, it creates a datagram socket. If the application has specified a client socket implementation factory, that factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Otherwise a system-default socket implementation is created. If there is a security manager, its checkConnect method is called with host.getHostAddress() and port as its arguments. This could result in a SecurityException. If UDP socket is used, TCP/IP related socket options will not apply.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: the IP address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the port number.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: if true, create a stream socket; otherwise, create a datagram socket.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the socket.
if a security manager exists and its checkConnect method doesn't allow the operation.
if the port parameter is outside the specified range of valid port values, which is between 0 and 65535, inclusive.
if host is null.

methods.name: connect
methods.signature: public void connect(SocketAddress endpoint) throws IOException
methods.description: Connects this socket to the server.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: the SocketAddress

throws.description: if an error occurs during the connection
if this socket has an associated channel, and the channel is in non-blocking mode
if endpoint is null or is a SocketAddress subclass not supported by this socket

methods.name: connect
methods.signature: public void connect(SocketAddress endpoint, int timeout) throws IOException
methods.description: Connects this socket to the server with a specified timeout value. A timeout of zero is interpreted as an infinite timeout. The connection will then block until established or an error occurs.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: the SocketAddress

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: the timeout value to be used in milliseconds.

throws.description: if an error occurs during the connection
if timeout expires before connecting
if this socket has an associated channel, and the channel is in non-blocking mode
if endpoint is null or is a SocketAddress subclass not supported by this socket, or if timeout is negative

methods.name: bind
methods.signature: public void bind(SocketAddress bindpoint) throws IOException
methods.description: Binds the socket to a local address. If the address is null, then the system will pick up an ephemeral port and a valid local address to bind the socket.

parameters.name: bindpoint
parameters.description: the SocketAddress to bind to

throws.description: if the bind operation fails, or if the socket is already bound.
if bindpoint is a SocketAddress subclass not supported by this socket
if a security manager exists and its checkListen method doesn't allow the bind to the local port.

methods.name: getInetAddress
methods.description: Returns the address to which the socket is connected. If the socket was connected prior to being closed, then this method will continue to return the connected address after the socket is closed.
methods.returns: the remote IP address to which this socket is connected, or null if the socket is not connected.

methods.name: getLocalAddress
methods.description: Gets the local address to which the socket is bound. If there is a security manager set, its checkConnect method is called with the local address and -1 as its arguments to see if the operation is allowed. If the operation is not allowed, the loopback address is returned.
methods.returns: the local address to which the socket is bound, the loopback address if denied by the security manager, or the wildcard address if the socket is closed or not bound yet.

methods.name: getPort
methods.description: Returns the remote port number to which this socket is connected. If the socket was connected prior to being closed, then this method will continue to return the connected port number after the socket is closed.
methods.returns: the remote port number to which this socket is connected, or 0 if the socket is not connected yet.

methods.name: getLocalPort
methods.description: Returns the local port number to which this socket is bound. If the socket was bound prior to being closed, then this method will continue to return the local port number after the socket is closed.
methods.returns: the local port number to which this socket is bound or -1 if the socket is not bound yet.

methods.name: getRemoteSocketAddress
methods.description: Returns the address of the endpoint this socket is connected to, or null if it is unconnected. If the socket was connected prior to being closed, then this method will continue to return the connected address after the socket is closed.
methods.returns: a SocketAddress representing the remote endpoint of this socket, or null if it is not connected yet.

methods.name: getLocalSocketAddress
methods.description: Returns the address of the endpoint this socket is bound to. If a socket bound to an endpoint represented by an InetSocketAddress is closed, then this method will continue to return an InetSocketAddress after the socket is closed. In that case the returned InetSocketAddress's address is the wildcard address and its port is the local port that it was bound to. If there is a security manager set, its checkConnect method is called with the local address and -1 as its arguments to see if the operation is allowed. If the operation is not allowed, a SocketAddress representing the loopback address and the local port to which this socket is bound is returned.
methods.returns: a SocketAddress representing the local endpoint of this socket, or a SocketAddress representing the loopback address if denied by the security manager, or null if the socket is not bound yet.

methods.name: getChannel
methods.description: Returns the unique SocketChannel object associated with this socket, if any. A socket will have a channel if, and only if, the channel itself was created via the SocketChannel.open or ServerSocketChannel.accept methods.
methods.returns: the socket channel associated with this socket, or null if this socket was not created for a channel

methods.name: getInputStream
methods.description: Returns an input stream for this socket. If this socket has an associated channel then the resulting input stream delegates all of its operations to the channel. If the channel is in non-blocking mode then the input stream's read operations will throw an IllegalBlockingModeException. Under abnormal conditions the underlying connection may be broken by the remote host or the network software (for example a connection reset in the case of TCP connections). When a broken connection is detected by the network software the following applies to the returned input stream :- The network software may discard bytes that are buffered by the socket. Bytes that aren't discarded by the network software can be read using read. If there are no bytes buffered on the socket, or all buffered bytes have been consumed by read, then all subsequent calls to read will throw an IOException. If there are no bytes buffered on the socket, and the socket has not been closed using close, then available will return 0. Closing the returned InputStream will close the associated socket.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the input stream, the socket is closed, the socket is not connected, or the socket input has been shutdown using shutdownInput()

methods.returns: an input stream for reading bytes from this socket.

methods.name: getOutputStream
methods.description: Returns an output stream for this socket. If this socket has an associated channel then the resulting output stream delegates all of its operations to the channel. If the channel is in non-blocking mode then the output stream's write operations will throw an IllegalBlockingModeException. Closing the returned OutputStream will close the associated socket.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when creating the output stream or if the socket is not connected.

methods.returns: an output stream for writing bytes to this socket.

methods.name: setTcpNoDelay
methods.signature: public void setTcpNoDelay(boolean on) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable TCP_NODELAY (disable/enable Nagle's algorithm).

parameters.name: on
parameters.description: true to enable TCP_NODELAY, false to disable.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.name: getTcpNoDelay
methods.description: Tests if TCP_NODELAY is enabled.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: a boolean indicating whether or not TCP_NODELAY is enabled.

methods.name: setSoLinger
methods.signature: public void setSoLinger(boolean on, int linger) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable SO_LINGER with the specified linger time in seconds. The maximum timeout value is platform specific. The setting only affects socket close.

parameters.name: on
parameters.description: whether or not to linger on.

parameters.name: linger
parameters.description: how long to linger for, if on is true.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.
if the linger value is negative.

methods.name: getSoLinger
methods.description: Returns setting for SO_LINGER. -1 returns implies that the option is disabled. The setting only affects socket close.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: the setting for SO_LINGER.

methods.name: sendUrgentData
methods.signature: public void sendUrgentData(int data) throws IOException
methods.description: Send one byte of urgent data on the socket. The byte to be sent is the lowest eight bits of the data parameter. The urgent byte is sent after any preceding writes to the socket OutputStream and before any future writes to the OutputStream.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The byte of data to send

throws.description: if there is an error sending the data.

methods.name: setOOBInline
methods.signature: public void setOOBInline(boolean on) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable SO_OOBINLINE (receipt of TCP urgent data) By default, this option is disabled and TCP urgent data received on a socket is silently discarded. If the user wishes to receive urgent data, then this option must be enabled. When enabled, urgent data is received inline with normal data. Note, only limited support is provided for handling incoming urgent data. In particular, no notification of incoming urgent data is provided and there is no capability to distinguish between normal data and urgent data unless provided by a higher level protocol.

parameters.name: on
parameters.description: true to enable SO_OOBINLINE, false to disable.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.name: getOOBInline
methods.description: Tests if SO_OOBINLINE is enabled.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: a boolean indicating whether or not SO_OOBINLINE is enabled.

methods.name: setSoTimeout
methods.signature: public void setSoTimeout(int timeout) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable SO_TIMEOUT with the specified timeout, in milliseconds. With this option set to a positive timeout value, a read() call on the InputStream associated with this Socket will block for only this amount of time. If the timeout expires, a java.net.SocketTimeoutException is raised, though the Socket is still valid. A timeout of zero is interpreted as an infinite timeout. The option must be enabled prior to entering the blocking operation to have effect.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: the specified timeout, in milliseconds.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error
if timeout is negative

methods.name: getSoTimeout
methods.description: Returns setting for SO_TIMEOUT. 0 returns implies that the option is disabled (i.e., timeout of infinity).

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: the setting for SO_TIMEOUT

methods.name: setSendBufferSize
methods.signature: public void setSendBufferSize(int size) throws SocketException
methods.description: Sets the SO_SNDBUF option to the specified value for this Socket. The SO_SNDBUF option is used by the platform's networking code as a hint for the size to set the underlying network I/O buffers. Because SO_SNDBUF is a hint, applications that want to verify what size the buffers were set to should call getSendBufferSize().

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: the size to which to set the send buffer size. This value must be greater than 0.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.
if the value is 0 or is negative.

methods.name: getSendBufferSize
methods.description: Get value of the SO_SNDBUF option for this Socket, that is the buffer size used by the platform for output on this Socket.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: the value of the SO_SNDBUF option for this Socket.

methods.name: setReceiveBufferSize
methods.signature: public void setReceiveBufferSize(int size) throws SocketException
methods.description: Sets the SO_RCVBUF option to the specified value for this Socket. The SO_RCVBUF option is used by the platform's networking code as a hint for the size to set the underlying network I/O buffers. Increasing the receive buffer size can increase the performance of network I/O for high-volume connection, while decreasing it can help reduce the backlog of incoming data. Because SO_RCVBUF is a hint, applications that want to verify what size the buffers were set to should call getReceiveBufferSize(). The value of SO_RCVBUF is also used to set the TCP receive window that is advertised to the remote peer. Generally, the window size can be modified at any time when a socket is connected. However, if a receive window larger than 64K is required then this must be requested before the socket is connected to the remote peer. There are two cases to be aware of: For sockets accepted from a ServerSocket, this must be done by calling ServerSocket.setReceiveBufferSize(int) before the ServerSocket is bound to a local address. For client sockets, setReceiveBufferSize() must be called before connecting the socket to its remote peer.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: the size to which to set the receive buffer size. This value must be greater than 0.

throws.description: if the value is 0 or is negative.
if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.name: getReceiveBufferSize
methods.description: Gets the value of the SO_RCVBUF option for this Socket, that is the buffer size used by the platform for input on this Socket.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: the value of the SO_RCVBUF option for this Socket.

methods.name: setKeepAlive
methods.signature: public void setKeepAlive(boolean on) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable SO_KEEPALIVE.

parameters.name: on
parameters.description: whether or not to have socket keep alive turned on.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.name: getKeepAlive
methods.description: Tests if SO_KEEPALIVE is enabled.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: a boolean indicating whether or not SO_KEEPALIVE is enabled.

methods.name: setTrafficClass
methods.signature: public void setTrafficClass(int tc) throws SocketException
methods.description: Sets traffic class or type-of-service octet in the IP header for packets sent from this Socket. As the underlying network implementation may ignore this value applications should consider it a hint. The tc must be in the range 0 <= tc <= 255 or an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. Notes: For Internet Protocol v4 the value consists of an integer, the least significant 8 bits of which represent the value of the TOS octet in IP packets sent by the socket. RFC 1349 defines the TOS values as follows: IPTOS_LOWCOST (0x02) IPTOS_RELIABILITY (0x04) IPTOS_THROUGHPUT (0x08) IPTOS_LOWDELAY (0x10) The last low order bit is always ignored as this corresponds to the MBZ (must be zero) bit. Setting bits in the precedence field may result in a SocketException indicating that the operation is not permitted. As RFC 1122 section 4.2.4.2 indicates, a compliant TCP implementation should, but is not required to, let application change the TOS field during the lifetime of a connection. So whether the type-of-service field can be changed after the TCP connection has been established depends on the implementation in the underlying platform. Applications should not assume that they can change the TOS field after the connection. For Internet Protocol v6 tc is the value that would be placed into the sin6_flowinfo field of the IP header.

parameters.name: tc
parameters.description: an int value for the bitset.

throws.description: if there is an error setting the traffic class or type-of-service

methods.name: getTrafficClass
methods.description: Gets traffic class or type-of-service in the IP header for packets sent from this Socket As the underlying network implementation may ignore the traffic class or type-of-service set using setTrafficClass(int) this method may return a different value than was previously set using the setTrafficClass(int) method on this Socket.

throws.description: if there is an error obtaining the traffic class or type-of-service value.

methods.returns: the traffic class or type-of-service already set

methods.name: setReuseAddress
methods.signature: public void setReuseAddress(boolean on) throws SocketException
methods.description: Enable/disable the SO_REUSEADDR socket option. When a TCP connection is closed the connection may remain in a timeout state for a period of time after the connection is closed (typically known as the TIME_WAIT state or 2MSL wait state). For applications using a well known socket address or port it may not be possible to bind a socket to the required SocketAddress if there is a connection in the timeout state involving the socket address or port. Enabling SO_REUSEADDR prior to binding the socket using bind(SocketAddress) allows the socket to be bound even though a previous connection is in a timeout state. When a Socket is created the initial setting of SO_REUSEADDR is disabled. The behaviour when SO_REUSEADDR is enabled or disabled after a socket is bound (See isBound()) is not defined.

parameters.name: on
parameters.description: whether to enable or disable the socket option

throws.description: if an error occurs enabling or disabling the SO_REUSEADDR socket option, or the socket is closed.

methods.name: getReuseAddress
methods.description: Tests if SO_REUSEADDR is enabled.

throws.description: if there is an error in the underlying protocol, such as a TCP error.

methods.returns: a boolean indicating whether or not SO_REUSEADDR is enabled.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Closes this socket. Any thread currently blocked in an I/O operation upon this socket will throw a SocketException. Once a socket has been closed, it is not available for further networking use (i.e. can't be reconnected or rebound). A new socket needs to be created. Closing this socket will also close the socket's InputStream and OutputStream. If this socket has an associated channel then the channel is closed as well.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when closing this socket.

methods.name: shutdownInput
methods.description: Places the input stream for this socket at "end of stream". Any data sent to the input stream side of the socket is acknowledged and then silently discarded. If you read from a socket input stream after invoking this method on the socket, the stream's available method will return 0, and its read methods will return -1 (end of stream).

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when shutting down this socket.

methods.name: shutdownOutput
methods.description: Disables the output stream for this socket. For a TCP socket, any previously written data will be sent followed by TCP's normal connection termination sequence. If you write to a socket output stream after invoking shutdownOutput() on the socket, the stream will throw an IOException.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when shutting down this socket.

methods.name: toString
methods.description: Converts this socket to a String.
methods.returns: a string representation of this socket.

methods.name: isConnected
methods.description: Returns the connection state of the socket. Note: Closing a socket doesn't clear its connection state, which means this method will return true for a closed socket (see isClosed()) if it was successfully connected prior to being closed.
methods.returns: true if the socket was successfully connected to a server

methods.name: isBound
methods.description: Returns the binding state of the socket. Note: Closing a socket doesn't clear its binding state, which means this method will return true for a closed socket (see isClosed()) if it was successfully bound prior to being closed.
methods.returns: true if the socket was successfully bound to an address

methods.name: isClosed
methods.description: Returns the closed state of the socket.
methods.returns: true if the socket has been closed

methods.name: isInputShutdown
methods.description: Returns whether the read-half of the socket connection is closed.
methods.returns: true if the input of the socket has been shutdown

methods.name: isOutputShutdown
methods.description: Returns whether the write-half of the socket connection is closed.
methods.returns: true if the output of the socket has been shutdown

methods.name: setSocketImplFactory
methods.signature: @Deprecated(since="17") public static void setSocketImplFactory(SocketImplFactory fac) throws IOException
methods.description: Sets the client socket implementation factory for the application. The factory can be specified only once. When an application creates a new client socket, the socket implementation factory's createSocketImpl method is called to create the actual socket implementation. Passing null to the method is a no-op unless the factory was already set. If there is a security manager, this method first calls the security manager's checkSetFactory method to ensure the operation is allowed. This could result in a SecurityException.

parameters.name: fac
parameters.description: the desired factory.

throws.description: if an I/O error occurs when setting the socket factory.
if the factory is already defined.
if a security manager exists and its checkSetFactory method doesn't allow the operation.

methods.name: setPerformancePreferences
methods.signature: public void setPerformancePreferences(int connectionTime, int latency, int bandwidth)
methods.description: Sets performance preferences for this socket. Sockets use the TCP/IP protocol by default. Some implementations may offer alternative protocols which have different performance characteristics than TCP/IP. This method allows the application to express its own preferences as to how these tradeoffs should be made when the implementation chooses from the available protocols. Performance preferences are described by three integers whose values indicate the relative importance of short connection time, low latency, and high bandwidth. The absolute values of the integers are irrelevant; in order to choose a protocol the values are simply compared, with larger values indicating stronger preferences. Negative values represent a lower priority than positive values. If the application prefers short connection time over both low latency and high bandwidth, for example, then it could invoke this method with the values (1, 0, 0). If the application prefers high bandwidth above low latency, and low latency above short connection time, then it could invoke this method with the values (0, 1, 2). Invoking this method after this socket has been connected will have no effect.

parameters.name: connectionTime
parameters.description: An int expressing the relative importance of a short connection time

parameters.name: latency
parameters.description: An int expressing the relative importance of low latency

parameters.name: bandwidth
parameters.description: An int expressing the relative importance of high bandwidth

methods.name: setOption
methods.signature: public <T> Socket setOption(SocketOption<T> name, T value) throws IOException
methods.description: Sets the value of a socket option.

parameters.name: T
parameters.description: The type of the socket option value

throws.description: if the socket does not support the option.
if the value is not valid for the option.
if an I/O error occurs, or if the socket is closed.
if name is null

if a security manager is set and if the socket option requires a security permission and if the caller does not have the required permission. StandardSocketOptions do not require any security permission.

methods.returns: this Socket

methods.name: getOption
methods.signature: public <T> T getOption(SocketOption<T> name) throws IOException
methods.description: Returns the value of a socket option.

parameters.name: T
parameters.description: The type of the socket option value

throws.description: if the socket does not support the option.
if an I/O error occurs, or if the socket is closed.
if name is null

if a security manager is set and if the socket option requires a security permission and if the caller does not have the required permission. StandardSocketOptions do not require any security permission.

methods.returns: The value of the socket option.

methods.name: supportedOptions
methods.description: Returns a set of the socket options supported by this socket. This method will continue to return the set of options even after the socket has been closed.
methods.returns: A set of the socket options supported by this socket. This set may be empty if the socket's SocketImpl cannot be created.
